auth_type: API Key or OAuth2 via Gateway
auth_notes: Traditional landlines (POTS/PSTN) have no native API. Integration requires a SIP/VoIP Gateway (e.g., Twilio, Telnyx, or a local Asterisk/FreePBX server) to bridge the physical line to a digital interface. Authentication is managed by the gateway provider.
rate_limits: Varies by gateway provider; typically limited by the number of physical channels/lines available (usually 1 concurrent call per standard analog line).
gotchas: Requires physical hardware (ATA or Gateway) or porting the number to a cloud provider to enable digital interaction.
Legacy infrastructure lacks metadata like caller ID name (CNAM) unless specifically provided by the carrier.
Real-time interaction (IVR) requires low-latency streaming which is difficult to manage via standard HTTP Request nodes.
client_must_provide: SIP Trunk credentials or CPaaS Account SID/Auth Token
Physical Analog Telephone Adapter (ATA) if keeping existing copper lines
operations_available: Trigger Workflow on Inbound Call (via Gateway Webhook)
research_notes: Direct integration with a copper wire is impossible. The HTTP Request node must target a middle-man API (like Twilio) or a self-hosted PBX (like Asterisk with ARI) that interfaces with the physical landline via a FXO gateway.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Direct integration with a copper wire is impossible. The HTTP Request node must target a middle-man API (like Twilio) or a self-hosted PBX (like Asterisk with ARI) that interfaces with the physical landline via a FXO gateway.
